question: Can client_registry_v1.json safely become the canonical merchant lifecycle authority?
exact_blocker_to_operational_revenue: client_registry_v1.json does not yet contain the full merchant lifecycle fields or linked lead/opportunity/delivery/action identifiers needed to unify lead through case-study truth; payment and fulfillment also remain split into separate runtime authorities.

conflicting_identifiers.finding: The same merchant identity is represented through different lifecycle layers, but client_registry does not own the lead and opportunity identifiers needed to unify them.
client_id is shared across delivery and opportunity truth, but the client registry does not yet encode those linked object ids as first-class merchant lifecycle references.
multiple lifecycle vocabularies exist (lead lifecycle, opportunity stage, delivery status, client lifecycle), so client_registry alone cannot currently be the canonical lifecycle authority.

duplicate_ownership.finding: Merchant identity is split across acquisition, client, opportunity, delivery, and commercial mapping layers.
ShopiFixer lifecycle is already modeled in multiple read layers, so client_registry cannot safely absorb it without schema expansion and a consolidation plan.
Client registry records merchant-level payment, but revenue truth still holds separate funnel-level revenue state.

why_no: client_registry_v1.json is canonical for clients, but it is not yet a full merchant lifecycle object.
It does not own acquisition truth, opportunity truth, or delivery truth.
It does not encode proof package, review, referral, or case-study readiness as first-class lifecycle state.
Payment and fulfillment are still split across runtime/fulfillment authorities.
A stale seeded commercial merchant registry still exists and competes conceptually with the client registry.

smallest_viable_authority_object.name: canonical merchant lifecycle object
required_shape: merchant identity: merchant_id, client_id, merchant_shop, store_domain, lead_id, opportunity_id
lifecycle: lead_status, audit_status, offer_status, payment_status, fulfillment_status, proof_package_status, revenue_status, case_study_status
commercial summary: offer_price, payment_amount, payment_currency, lifetime_value, current_stage, next_required_action, readiness_score
smallest_viable_authority_object.reason: That is the smallest object that can unify the merchant across lead, audit, offer, payment, fulfillment, proof, revenue, and case-study paths without creating another competing registry.
